elements.text: 6. Let ri denote the least nonnegative residue of 10i (mod 7). Compute ri for i = 1, .. . , 6. Compute the decimal expansion of the fraction 1/7 without using a calculator. Can you find where the numbers r1, . . . , r6 appear in the process of dividing 7 into 1?

elements.text: 7. Compute the order of 10 modulo 13. Compute the period of the frac- tion 1/13.

elements.text: 8. Let p be prime and a an integer not divisible by p. Prove that if a2n = −1 (mod p), then a has order 2n+1 modulo p.

elements.text: 9. Let m be a positive integer not divisible by 2 or 5. Prove that the decimal expansion of the fraction 1/m is periodic with period equal to the order of 10 modulo m.

elements.text: 10. Prove that the decimal expansion of 1/m is finite if and only if the prime divisors of m are 2 and 5.

elements.text: 11. Prove that 10 has order 22 modulo 23. Deduce that the decimal ex- pansion of 1/23 has period 22.
